webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
March shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Jimmy Carr from Friday 13 to Sunday 15 March. Shows start nightly at 7.30pm
  • Big Time Rush on Tuesday 17 March. Shows starts 7.30pm
  • Old Dominion on Wednesday 18 March. Show starts at 7pm
  • Mel Robbins on Wednesday 25 March. Show starts at 7.30pm
  • Jeff Chang on Saturday 28 March. Show starts 7.30pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Royal Australian Navy International Fleet Review will be held across Sydney Harbour on Saturday 21 March. The Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review 2026 will see the largest gathering of international warships on Sydney Harbour for more than a decade with up to 30 ships from 20 countries assembling in an impressive display of international sea power. 

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Special event clearways will be in effect from 6am Saturday 21 March to 6am Wednesday 25 March on Cowper Wharf Roadway western side between Dowling St and Wylde St

Clearways are strict no parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 28 Mar to 2am Mon 30 Mar at the following locations to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Railway St, southbound between Day St & Help St,
  • Railway St, northbound between Brown St & Help St
  • Brown St, westbound between Railway St & Pacific Hwy.

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains Trackwork

properties.publicTransport: Route 389 and 501 buses will be diverting around road closures. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures and special event clearways will be in place for the City of Sydney's Sydney Streets event on Harris St. The event runs from 11am until 10pm. 

Motorists in the area should allow extra travel time, follow the directions of traffic controllers and be aware of increased pedestrian activity.

Road closures will be in place from 7am until 11:30pm on:

  • Harris St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and John St
  • Miller St between Little Mount St and Harris St
  • Sections of other local roads will also be closed including Bulwara Rd, Jones St, Little Mount St, Miller Ln, Saunders St and Union St.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Harris St and Miller St. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
ROAD CLOSURES

PYRMONT

FROM 7AM TO 11:30PM

  • Bulwara Rd* Off Miller St
  • Harris St Between John St and Pyrmont Bridge Rd
  • Jones St* Off Miller St
  • Little Mount St* Between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and Miller St
  • Miller Lane* Off Miller St
  • Miller St Between Little Mount St and Paternoster Row
  • Miller St Between Little Mount St and Harris St
  • Miller St* Between Saunders St and Little Mount St
  • Mount St* Off Miller St
  • Saunders St* Between Quarry Master Dr and Miller St (southbound)
  • Union St Between Harris St and Paternoster Row

*Local Resident Access only


SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

Special event clearways will be in effect on both sides of the roads below unless stated otherwise. Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and also apply to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, call 132 701 to find its new location.

FROM 7AM UNTIL 11:30PM

  • Harris Street between Pyrmont Bridge Road and John Street
  • Miller Street between Little Mount Street and Harris Street
arrangementElements.title: Road closures & Special event clearways

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on Sunday 29 March on the T1 North Shore Line, Sydney Metro line (between Victoria Cross and Macquarie Park) and the City Circle. Use Light rail services or Metro to get within walking distance of the start line. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Run2Cure fun run events will take place in The Domain starting from 6.30am finishing around 1pm. Approximately 3,500 participants are expected.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching The Domain as participants travel to and from the event.

Road closures: Art Gallery Rd and Mrs Macquaries Rd will be closed from 5am to 11.30am. No vehicle access or parking will be permitted until roads are reopened.

Hospital Rd will remain open with limited parking and a drop off zone located immediately south of the pedestrian crossing on the corner of Hospital Rd and Phillip Path. Parking is available in the Domain car park.

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on Sunday 29 March on the T1 North Shore Line, Sydney Metro line (between Victoria Cross and Macquarie Park) and the City Circle. Bikes are not permitted to be taken on-board buses including rail replacement buses. Trains continue to run to Hornsby on the T9 Northern Line. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 3,000 participants are expected to take part in the annual Bobbin Head Cycle Classic. Events start and finish at Karuah Oval in Turramurra, from 6.30am. All events will finish by 1.30pm. Changed traffic conditions will be in effect along the course for the safety of participants.

  • Road and lane closures will be in place along the course at various locations and times between 5am and 3pm, including on sections of Karuah Rd, Turramurra Ave, Bobbin Head Rd, Warrimoo Ave, Woodbury Rd, Liberator General San Martin Dr, Old Pacific Hwy, Mona Vale Rd and Burns Rd through Turramurra, Ku-Ring-Gai Chase National Park, Mt Colah, St Ives and Terrey Hills. 
  • Speed limit reductions will be in effect at key locations along the course at various times between 6am and 2pm.

A special event clearway will also be in place on the eastern side of Mona Vale Rd in St Ives from 7am until 1.30pm, between 425 Mona Vale Rd and Kitchener St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
ROAD CLOSURES

TURRAMURRA - FROM 5AM TO 3PM

  • Karuah Road between Eastern Road and Turramurra Avenue
  • Turramurra Avenue between Brentwood Avenue and Karuah Road (northbound)

KU-RING-GAI CHASE NATIONAL PARK

FROM 6.15AM TO 9.15AM

  • Bobbin Head Road between the park gates at North Turramurra and Mt Colah (northbound)

FROM 7.30AM TO 12.30PM

LANE CLOSURES

MOUNT COLAH - FROM 6.15AM TO 8.45AM

  • Lane 1 Old Pacific Highway approaching the intersection of Belmont Parade (both directions)

TURRAMURRA

FROM 6.45AM TO 1.30PM

  • Right turn lane Burns Road at the intersection of Ellalong Road (northbound)

FROM 8AM TO 1.45PM

TERREY HILLS

FROM 8:15AM TO 1:00PM

  • Booralie Road, between McCarrs Creek Road and Mona Vale Rd access road, (westbound)


ST IVES

FROM 6.30AM to 2.30PM

  • Lane 1 Mona Vale Road approaching the intersection of Richmond Avenue (westbound)

FROM 8AM TO 1.30PM

  • Right turn lane Mona Vale Road approaching the intersection of Woodbury Road (northbound)


SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

Special event Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

ST IVES - FROM 7AM TO 1.30PM

  • Mona Vale Road between 425 Mona Vale Road and Kitchener Street (southbound)
arrangementElements.title: Road and lane closures

arrangementElements.html:
Reduced speed limits will be in effect on the following roads to ensure the safety of cyclists sharing roads with motorists:

ST IVES TO TERREY HILLS

FROM 6AM TO 11.30AM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Mona Vale Road between Wildflower Gardens at St Ives to Myoora Road at Terrey Hills (eastbound)

FROM 6AM TO 2PM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Mona Vale Road from McCarrs Creek Road at Terrey Hills to opposite the Wildflower Garden at St Ives (westbound)

KU-RING-GAI CHASE - FROM 6AM TO 1PM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on West Head Road between Liberator General San Martin Drive and West Head Picnic Area (both directions)

MT COLAH - FROM 6AM TO 10AM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Bobbin Head Road between Park Gates and Kalkari Visitor Centre (eastbound)

MT COLAH - FROM 5:30AM TO 9:30AM

  • 40km/h speed limit reduction on Old Pacific Highway between Pacific Highway and Belmont Parade (both directions)

TERREY HILLS - FROM 6AM TO 11.30AM 

  • 40km/h speed limit reduction on McCarr's Creek Road between West Head Road and Liberator General San Martin Drive (westbound)

roads.suburb: Turramurra, St Ives, Ku-Ring-Gai, Terrey Hills, Mt Colah

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Netball Double Header at Qudos Bank Arena. Game 1 - Giants v Melbourne Vixens: 3pm. Game 2 - NSW Swifts v Melbourne Mavericks: 5pm, finish at 6.30pm

Traffic delays are expected as netball fans make their way to Olympic Park.

Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you have to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Dragons at CommBank Stadium. NSW Cup game at 1.35pm, NRL kick off at 4.05pm, full time at 5.50pm

With thousands expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 5.50pm to 6.20pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on City Circle, metro and T1 North Shore Line. Race day tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Family Stakes Day at Rosehill Gardens Racecourse. First race at 12.30pm, last race from 5:55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the racecourse.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect at the following locations:

  • From 6am to 6pm: Grand Avenue from 100m Prior to Gate 1 Australian Turf Club Rosehill Gardens to opposite Grand Avenue North (southern side).

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Additional route 199 buses will also run towards Manly and Mona Vale after the game to help you get home. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sea Eagles v Roosters at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. Prelim matches from 5.30pm. NRL kick-off at 8pm and full time around 9.50pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand, and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run between the city and Watsons Bay. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Royal Australian Navy International Fleet Review will be held across Sydney Harbour on Saturday 21 March. The Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review 2026 will see the largest gathering of international warships on Sydney Harbour for more than a decade with up to 30 ships from 20 countries assembling in an impressive display of international sea power. 

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching Watsons Bay as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Special event clearways will be in effect from 10am to 6pm Saturday 21 March on Military Road between Robertson Place and bus turning circle (north of Gap Road) western side, and Robertson Pl between Old South Head Rd and Military Rd northern side.

Clearways are strict no parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Race day tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Golden Slipper Day at Rosehill Gardens Racecourse. First race at 12.30pm, last race from 5:55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the racecourse.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect at the following locations:

  • From 6am to 6pm: Grand Avenue from 100m Prior to Gate 1 Australian Turf Club Rosehill Gardens to opposite Grand Avenue North (southern side).

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

No access to Sampson St from Mitchell St.

Sampson Street (local road) will be closed from 07:00 to 19:00. Local resident access is permitted during the event setup from 07:00 to 11:30. Sampson Street will be closed to all traffic from 11:30 to 18:00. 

---

Attendees traveling to Orange from Sydney or Penrith should be aware of the current closure of the Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass. A detour is in place via Darling Causeway and Chifley Rd, Lithgow.

Motorists are advised to plan your trip and to allow plenty of additional travel time.

For more information, please visit Great Western Highway Closure.
properties.diversions: Detour via Mitchell Hwy, Woodward St or Clinton St, and Byng St.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT Sampson Street Long Lunch

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on all modes of public transport. Route 525 and 526 bus services will be diverting while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
AFL: GWS Giants v St Kilda at ENGIE Stadium in Olympic Park. First Bounce at 4.15pm and full time at 6.45pm.

  • Sydney Olympic Park will be very busy Saturday evening with crowds also heading to the AFC Women’s Asian Cup Grand Final and an NBL Championship Final match at nearby stadium. AFL ticketholders are urged to leave the car at home to avoid extensive delays leaving the match. 
  • Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • ** PARKING SOLD OUT ** All available pre-booked parking spaces in Olympic Park have sold out for Saturday 21 March.
  • If you have pre-booked parking, follow directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • If you haven't pre-booked parking do not drive to Olympic Park. You will need to catch public transport as there will be no parking available on-site. You can also consider parking at a nearby commuter car park and continuing your trip by train or bus to Olympic Park.
  • Road closures: local roads are closed in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on trains. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sharks v Dolphins at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. Kick-off at 5.30pm, full time around 7.15pm.

Road closures may be in place on Captain Cook Dr from approximately 10 minutes before kick-off and remain in place until crowds have cleared around 30 minutes after full-time.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd. 

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Rabbitohs v West Tigers at polytec Stadium. Jersey Flegg kick off: 3.25pm, NSW Cup kick off: 5.05pm, NRL kick off: 7.30pm, Full time: 8.50pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to and from the match.

Allow extra travel time and use local parking. 

Dane Drive will be closed from 2.15pm between the Central Coast Highway and Donnison Street and will reopen once crowds have cleared the area.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Rugby: Waratahs v Blues at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car park opens from 4.35pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 11pm on sections of Cleveland Street.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 11pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to 11pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side)

properties.publicTransport: Route 389 buses is diverting around Stanley St while it's closed for the event. Check bus service alerts and plan your trip at transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures and special event clearways are in place for the City of Sydney – Sydney Streets event on Stanley Street. The event runs from 11am until 10pm. 

Roads closures are in place since 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Stanley St, Riley St, Chapel St, Francis St, Yurong St and other local roads in the area.

Allow extra travel time, follow the directions of traffic control and be aware of increased pedestrian activity.

Special event clearways are also in effect since 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Chapel St, Crown St, Riley St, Stanley St and Yurong St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

webLinks.linkText: Darling Harbour - AFC Women's Asian Cup Final live site

properties.publicTransport: L1 Light rail services run regularly to Exhibition Centre and Paddys Markets within walking distance of Tumbalong Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be open at Tumbalong Park in Darling Harbour on Saturday for the AFC Women's Asian Cup final match Japan v Australia.

The match will be live on the big screen and the site is free to visit. Match kicks off from 8pm.

Fans are encouraged to catch public transport.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads throughout the Sydney CBD.
properties.displayName: AFC Women's Asian Cup Final live site

properties.publicTransport: Some buses will be diverting, missing stops and using temporary stops while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The annual Camden Show will be held at Onslow Park and the Bicentennial Equestrian Park, from 8am to 11pm Friday 20 & Saturday 21 March. Last Gate entry is 9.30pm each night.

John Street will be closed between Mitchell Street and Exeter Street from 6am Friday to 11.30pm Saturday.

The Camden Street Parade will also be held from 4pm on Friday with rolling road closures.

Traffic may be heavier than usual throughout the day on roads approaching and around the Town Centre.

Motorists are advised to allow extra travel time, follow diversion signs and be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Tickets include travel on transport. Express trains and major event buses will run. Route 525 and 526 bus services will be diverting while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
AFC Women’s Asian Cup Final: Japan v Australia at Stadium Australia Olympic Park. Gates open 6pm, Tournament closing ceremony entertainment from 7.35pm, Match kick-off at 8pm, finish approx 9.45pm, subject to extra time and/or penalties.

  • Heavy traffic is expected as tens of thousands of fans make their way to Olympic Park to cheer on the Matildas in the Grand Final. Ticketholders are urged to leave the car at home and catch public to avoid traffic and car park delays.
  • The precinct will be busy with an AFL match and an NBL Finals match also being held at neighbouring venues in the precinct.
  • Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park, including on the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • ** PARKING SOLD OUT ** All available pre-booked parking spaces in Olympic Park have sold out for Saturday 21 March.
  • If you have pre-booked parking, follow directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • If you haven't pre-booked parking do not drive to Olympic Park. You will need to catch public transport as there will be no parking available on-site. You can also consider parking at a nearby commuter car park and continuing your trip by train or bus to Olympic Park.
  • Note P6 car park is closed due to Easter Show set up and a fenceline is in place, changing the way pedestrians move around the precinct.
  • Road closures: local roads are closed in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

arrangementElements.html:
See the Sydney Olympic Park road closure map for more details.

Grand Final road closures

From 1am Friday 20 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Showground Road
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Edwin Flack Ave

From 1.30am Saturday 21 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Olympic Boulevard from Herb Elliott Ave to Edwin Flack Avenue
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard Orana Parade
  • Showground Road from Grand Parade to Dawn Fraser Avenue
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Gate 15 to Showground Road 

From 12.00am Saturday 21 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Kevin Coombes Avenue between Pondage Link and Australia Avenue (eastbound) 

From 1pm Saturday 21 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Olympic Boulevard between Sarah Durack and Herb Elliott Avenue 

From 1.30pm Saturday 21 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Shirley Strickland between P4 Roundabout and Rod Laver Drive.
  • Rod Laver Drive at the rear of Tennis Centre to Shirley Strickland west

From 2pm Saturday 21 March – 3am Sunday 22 March 

  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Park Street to Showground Road.
  • Showground Road from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Rear of Novotel Loading Dock.

From 5pm Saturday 21 March – 1am Sunday 22 March

  • Kevin Coombs Avenue between Pondage Link and Australia Avenue (westbound)
  • Holker Busway from Hill Road to Australia Avenue

From 9pm Saturday 21 March – 1am Sunday 22 March

  • Edwin Flack Avenue from Pondage Link to Old Hill Link (westbound)
  • Edwin Flack Avenue northbound from Bernie Avenue to Old Hill Link
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Gate 15 to Australia Avenue
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Park Street to Australia Avenue
  • Herb Elliott Avenue closed between Park Street and Olympic Boulevard.
arrangementElements.title: Road closures

properties.displayName: AFC Women’s Asian Cup Final: Japan v Australia

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NBL Championship Series Game 3: Sydney Kings v Adelaide 36ers at Qudos Bank Arena on Sunday 29 March from 2.15pm. Game 5 will be played if required.

  • Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Olympic Park as basketball fans make their way to and from the match. 
  • Motorists should carpool, plan to arrive early and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry. Note that P6 car park will be closed due to Easter Show infrastructure.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.otherAdvice: Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow participants of the March in March for Forests to cross the Highway safely.
Waterfall Way (Hyde St) will be closed from 11am to 1pm for the Bob Brown Foundation - March for the Forests on Sunday 22 March.
properties.diversions: Detour will be via Prince St, Park St, Bowra St and Oak St.
properties.displayName: Bob Brown Foundation - March for Forests

properties.publicTransport: Tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Sydney FC v Newcastle Jets at Allianz Stadium. Kick off from 3pm, full time at 4.50pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should allow extra travel time on Anzac Pde, Lang Rd, Oxford St, Syd Einfeld Drive, Darley Rd, York Rd and Alison Rd.

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 1pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Park open from 12pm.

Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 1pm to 7pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 7pm on Cleveland Street.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator
